summary: Project to build an Android app that includes a driver-facing booking dispatch system.

existing_features.title: Create a driver dispatch UI that lets an authenticated driver view and manage their assigned bookings: include an “Assigned Bookings” list with key details (booking id, pickup/destination, pickup time, customer name/phone, current status), a detail panel, and clear actions to Accept, Refuse (with required reason), Mark Completed, and Cancel (with required reason).

Add navigation/routes for the driver dispatch workflow: introduce a route for the dispatch inbox (e.g., /driver/dispatch) and ensure drivers can easily jump from an assigned booking to the existing location update page (/driver/track/$bookingId). Add a prominent entry point from the Profile page (driver profile) to the dispatch inbox.

Add React Query hooks for driver dispatch: provide a query hook for fetching the caller’s assigned bookings and mutation hooks for driver status updates (and reason when applicable). Ensure query keys and invalidation patterns are consistent with existing booking/tracking hooks.

Apply a cohesive driver-dispatch visual theme consistent with the app’s existing warm/amber styling: clear hierarchy for “new/active/completed” states, high-contrast status badges, and mobile-first layout that works well in the Android build.

new_feature_requests.summary: Add booking dispatch system for driver (receive and manage booking assignments).

Add backend APIs to support a driver dispatch workflow: (1) allow an authenticated driver to fetch a list of bookings assigned to them, sorted by newest first, and (2) allow an assigned driver to update the status of their own booking to one of: accepted, refused, completed, or cancelled (and when cancelled/refused, store an optional reason). Enforce authorization so only the assigned driver (or admin where appropriate) can access these APIs.
